meta.name: Nova Chen
meta.title: Nova Chen — Spacecraft Software Engineer
meta.description: Building reliable systems for exploration — from ground control UIs to orbital telemetry pipelines.

hero.headline: Engineering software that orbits the impossible.
hero.roleLine: Senior full-stack engineer · mission systems & operator UIs
hero.subheadline: Full-stack developer focused on resilient systems, immersive interfaces, and tools that help teams ship under pressure.

about.title: About
about.subtitle: Exploration starts where comfort ends.
about.openTo: Open to full-time product engineering, consulting, and speaking on resilient systems.
body: I design and ship software where failure modes matter — telemetry dashboards, mission simulators, and developer platforms used in high-stakes environments.
When I'm not coding, I'm sketching star maps, contributing to open-source observability tools, and mentoring engineers entering aerospace-adjacent tech.

projects.title: Orbital Telemetry Console
projects.summary: Real-time satellite health dashboard with anomaly detection and operator playbooks.
projects.imageAlt: Orbital Telemetry Console dashboard showing satellite health metrics
body: Mission operators lacked a unified view of satellite health across multiple buses — alerts were scattered across tools, slowing incident response during critical windows.

I led frontend architecture for a real-time dashboard ingesting WebSocket telemetry streams, surfacing anomaly scores with configurable operator playbooks for acknowledge, escalate, and auto-remediate flows.
The console became the primary operator surface for 40+ users, cutting mean incident response time by 40% and establishing patterns reused across ground-system products.

projects.title: Nebula Design System
projects.summary: Accessible component library themed for dark environments and mission-critical UIs.
projects.imageAlt: Nebula Design System component library preview
body: Three product teams were shipping inconsistent UIs in low-light control rooms — accessibility gaps and ad-hoc styling slowed delivery and increased operator error risk.
I designed and documented a token-driven component library with Storybook workflows, contrast checklists, and dark-environment readability as first-class requirements.
Nebula became the shared foundation for mission-critical interfaces, adopted by three teams and reducing duplicate UI work across ground-system products.

projects.title: Launch Pad CI
projects.summary: Pipeline orchestration templates for multi-stage deploys with rollback guardrails.
projects.imageAlt: Launch Pad CI pipeline orchestration workflow
body: Services deployed with inconsistent CI gates — failed production releases were common and rollbacks required manual coordination across teams.
I created reusable GitHub Actions templates with staged deploy gates, automated smoke tests, and one-click rollback paths integrated into existing service repos.
Standardized pre-flight checks across 12 services reduced failed production deploys by 35% and gave teams a shared playbook for safe releases.

experience.summary: Ground-system UI and incident tooling for mission operations.
highlights: Led UI modernization for the telemetry console used by 40+ operators.
Reduced mean incident response time by 40% via playbooks and alerting.
Introduced design-system patterns adopted across three product teams.

experience.summary: Simulation tooling and APIs for pre-flight validation workflows.
highlights: Built simulation tooling and APIs for pre-flight validation workflows.
Delivered test harnesses that cut pre-launch verification cycles by 25%.

contact.title: Establish contact
contact.message: Send a message — I usually reply within two business days.
